words.w: Every summer, Bristol Bay's sockeye come back by the million.
For seventy years, people have counted them by hand,
ten minutes of every hour, around the clock.
That count sets the season, when to fish, and when to stop.
Now a drone lifts from a box at eight thirty,
and films the run from above.
But it cannot count. Not yet.
First, a person has to teach it,
marking each fish that crosses a line, several thousand of them.
Only then can the model pick the fish out on its own.
It has not been tested against the towers yet.
And a shadow on the river can still fool it.
So the counters still climb the towers,
and the model learns to watch beside them.
The hand still teaches. The count still comes home.
